short_summary: El Niño confirmed, wildfire risk rises
rationale: El Niño now officially confirmed active by scientists, directly raising wildfire and drought risk for Portugal this season — a tangible, fresh threat to daily life and the tourism-dependent economy.

top_events.title: El Niño officially confirmed active
top_events.summary: Scientists confirm El Niño is underway, threatening weather extremes; for Portugal this materially raises seasonal wildfire, drought and heatwave risk with direct impact on agriculture, tourism and daily life.

top_events.title: Trump claims US-Iran 'great settlement'
top_events.summary: If sustained, could ease EU energy prices slightly, marginally reducing Portuguese cost-of-living pressure.

top_events.title: Pope Leo visits Canary Islands on migration
top_events.summary: Highlights Atlantic migration routes; minor relevance for Portugal given proximity and shared Atlantic migration dynamics.
